# BoxGraph: Semantic Place Recognition and Pose Estimation from 3D LiDAR

- **Work ID:** `LPR-032`
- **Reviewed version:** `arxiv:2206.15154`
- **Year / venue:** 2022 / IROS
- **Relevance:** `core_lpr`
- **Representative tier:** `ordinary`
- **Links:** [Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.15154)

## Reviewed taxonomy assignments

- [map](../branches/map.md)
- [map/handcrafted](../branches/map--handcrafted.md)
- [semantics](../branches/semantics.md)
- [semantics/graph_based](../branches/semantics--graph_based.md)

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** SemanticKITTI/KITTI Odometry 00, 02, 05, 06, 07 and 08 — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–6, § Abstract;III. BoxGraph;IV. Evaluation, Fig. 2-7;Table I-III
- **input_unit:** single semantically segmented LiDAR scan matched against scans in a prebuilt map — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–4, § Abstract;III. BoxGraph;IV. Evaluation, Fig. 2-7;Table I-III
- **invariances:** inter-object edge distances are rigid-transform invariant, the full descriptor is not globally rotation invariant because bounding boxes are measured in the sensor frame and road-aligned heading is assumed — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 3–5, § Abstract;III. BoxGraph;IV. Evaluation, Fig. 2-7;Table I-III
- **representation:** DBSCAN semantic-object clusters, fully connected semantic graph, vertex centroid, class and sensor-frame 3-D bounding-box dimensions, inter-centroid-distance edges, Hungarian same-class assignment, RANSAC and SVD 6-DoF pose, inlier-refined graph similarity — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–5, § Abstract;III. BoxGraph;IV. Evaluation, Fig. 2-7;Table I-III
- **uses_learning:** no, the BoxGraph core is handcrafted and may consume labels from external learned segmenters — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–8, § Abstract;III. BoxGraph;IV. Evaluation, Fig. 2-7;Table I-III
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–8, § Abstract;III. BoxGraph;IV. Evaluation, Fig. 2-7;Table I-III

> Information types distinguish author-reported facts, derived facts, researcher judgements, and agent inferences. The locator identifies the reviewed source; verbatim evidence remains in the private audit store.
